title: Mark Your Territory
description: Use marks and the jumplist to navigate a complex codebase
tasks: Open app/src/pages/index.astro and set mark a (ma)
Open app/src/layouts/Base.astro and set mark b (mb)
Open app/src/components/DayNav.astro and set mark c (mc)
Jump to mark a ('a) — verify you're in index.astro
Jump to mark b ('b) — verify you're in Base.astro
Use the jumplist to go back (<C-o> twice)
Use the jumplist to go forward (<C-i>)
Jump to your last edit position (g;)
hints: Lowercase marks are per-buffer, uppercase marks are global (cross-file)

title: Text Object Surgery
description: Use advanced mini.ai text objects to restructure code
hints: Use daf to delete an entire function
Position cursor inside getUser, then daf deletes the whole function

title: Global Refactor
description: Use grug-far to perform a project-wide rename with regex
tasks: Open grug-far (<leader>sr)
Search for 'DayNav' across all .astro and .ts files
Replace with 'LessonNavigation'
Preview all changes before applying
Apply the changes
Check diagnostics (<leader>xx) to verify nothing broke
Undo the changes (this was practice)
hints: grug-far shows a preview of all changes before applying
Use file glob filters to limit scope
After applying, check diagnostics to catch any issues

title: The Final Flight
description: The ultimate challenge: combine all 30 days of skills in a timed workflow
tasks: STAGE 1 — Navigation (target: 30s): Flash-jump to 3 functions across 2 files, set marks at each, return to start via jumplist
STAGE 2 — Editing (target: 60s): Transform a data structure using operators, text objects, surround, and macros. Convert 5 raw strings to typed objects.
STAGE 3 — Refactoring (target: 60s): Rename a component with LSP, fix all diagnostics, format all changed files
STAGE 4 — Workflow (target: 60s): Stage changes with gitsigns, open lazygit, write commit message, push
STAGE 5 — Speed (target: 30s): Apply the same surround operation to 10 words using dot command and flash-jump
hints: Focus on smooth transitions between stages — don't rush individual operations
